title: Taiko Website Clone — Static Content, Pixel-Perfect Reproduction
overview: Clone the Taiko (taiko.xyz) website from the existing Next.js 14 + Strapi CMS codebase into a new, self-contained Next.js 15 (App Router) application where ALL content lives in the repo (no CMS). The result must be visually identical to the original, deployed on Vercel, and use the latest stable versions of all frameworks and libraries.
goals: All content stored as static data in the repo (no Strapi, no CMS)
Latest stable framework and library versions
Maintain all animations, responsive behavior, and interactive features
non_goals: Adding new features or pages not present in the original
Changing the visual design or branding
Building a CMS or admin interface
Internationalization beyond English (original only supports English)

content_architecture.strategy: All content that was previously fetched from Strapi CMS will be stored as static TypeScript/JSON data files within the repo. Blog posts will use MDX for rich content.

content_types.format: MDX files with frontmatter (title, slug, date, timeToRead, category, image)
MDX files with frontmatter (title, slug, type, location)

files: home.ts — homepage dynamic content (hero, solutions, about, governance, explore, roadmap)
alethia.ts — Alethia page data (hero, about, features, explore, cards, FAQ)
gwyneth.ts — Gwyneth page data (same structure as Alethia)
gwyneth-apps.ts — Gwyneth Apps page data (hero, about, dapps, apps list, disclaimer)
dao.ts — DAO page data (welcome, about, join, FAQ)

gsap.description: GSAP with ScrollTrigger for scroll-based animations throughout the site
gsap.usage: Register ScrollTrigger plugin globally. Used for parallax effects, reveal animations, section transitions.

lottie.description: Lottie animations for visual elements (intro hero, ecosystem, rocket, laptop, infrastructure icons)
lottie.usage: Use @lottiefiles/react-lottie-player to render JSON animation files

aos.description: Custom Animate On Scroll utility — adds '--animate' class when elements with 'aos' class enter viewport
aos.usage: Replicate the custom AOS utility from src/shared/lib/utils/aos.ts

transitions.description: React Transition Group for component enter/exit transitions

additional: Generate sitemap.xml (not present in original — add for improved SEO)
Use Next.js Metadata API (App Router) for all meta tags

Configure Vercel project and deploy
Verify all animations, transitions, and interactive elements work correctly

key_technical_decisions.decision: Next.js App Router instead of Pages Router
key_technical_decisions.rationale: The original uses Pages Router (Next.js 14). We upgrade to App Router (Next.js 15) for latest patterns, React Server Components, and better performance. This requires adapting getServerSideProps to server component data fetching and removing next-i18next dependency.

key_technical_decisions.decision: MDX for rich content instead of Strapi Blocks
key_technical_decisions.rationale: Blog posts and career positions used Strapi's BlocksContent format. MDX provides a natural, file-based alternative for rich content with React component support. Use @next/mdx or next-mdx-remote.

key_technical_decisions.decision: Static data files instead of React Query + API calls
key_technical_decisions.rationale: All content is now in-repo. Replace TanStack React Query + Axios data fetching layer with direct imports or file-system reads in Server Components. Pagination and filtering done at build time or via client-side filtering of static data.

key_technical_decisions.decision: Remove i18n framework since only English is supported
key_technical_decisions.rationale: The original uses next-i18next but only has English locale. Move translation strings directly into content data files or component-level constants. Eliminates unnecessary dependency.
The original's SCSS architecture (vars, mixins, modules) is well-structured. Maintain this approach rather than switching to Tailwind or CSS-in-JS to minimize visual divergence.
GSAP and ScrollTrigger are browser-only. Use 'use client' directive for components that need GSAP. Dynamically import GSAP to avoid SSR issues.

risks.risk: Strapi Blocks content format may be difficult to replicate in MDX
risks.mitigation: Build a conversion script that transforms Strapi Blocks JSON into MDX. For FAQ answers and complex rich text, use structured JSON data rendered through a custom RichText component.

risks.risk: GSAP ScrollTrigger may conflict with App Router streaming/hydration
risks.mitigation: Wrap all GSAP-dependent components in 'use client' boundary. Use dynamic imports with ssr: false where needed. Register ScrollTrigger in a client-side effect.

risks.risk: Some content may only exist in the Strapi database, not in source code
risks.mitigation: Use the live taiko.xyz site as reference to manually capture any content not found in source files. Blog posts, careers, events, and ecosystem data must be scraped or exported from Strapi.

risks.risk: Font licensing — custom fonts may have restrictions
risks.mitigation: Copy fonts as-is from original repo. The project already includes these fonts in the public directory.

risks.risk: Lottie animation files are large and may impact performance
risks.mitigation: Lazy load Lottie animations. Use dynamic imports and intersection observer to only load when visible.

acceptance_criteria: All 15 pages render and are visually identical to the original taiko.xyz
All content is stored in the repository — no external CMS or API dependencies
All GSAP scroll animations, Lottie animations, and transitions work correctly
Responsive design works across all breakpoints (mobile, tablet, laptop, desktop, ultrawide)
Navigation mega-menu works with correct dropdowns and mobile hamburger menu
Blog listing supports pagination and filtering by category and date
Ecosystem page supports search, type filter, and category filter
Events page supports location and month filtering
Grant program FAQ accordion works correctly
Brand assets page displays logos, colors, typography correctly with download links
Newsletter subscribe form renders (can be non-functional or redirect to external service)
SEO meta tags, OG tags, and Twitter cards are present on all pages
Fonts load correctly with proper preloading
No console errors in production build
qualityGates: pnpm run build must pass with no errors
No console errors in production build

stories.title: Project Scaffolding & Static Assets
stories.description: Initialize Next.js 15 project with App Router, TypeScript, SASS, pnpm. Configure tsconfig.json with path aliases (baseUrl: './src'). Copy all static assets (fonts, images, Lottie files, media, documents, favicon) from the source repo at /Users/gustavo/taiko/website-v3. Copy and adapt SCSS files (vars, mixins, reset, fonts, custom, style) from the source repo. Configure next.config.ts for SASS, image optimization, and Vercel deployment. Set up ESLint and Prettier configuration. Install all required dependencies: gsap, @gsap/react, @lottiefiles/react-lottie-player, react-transition-group, clsx, dayjs, swiper, use-debounce, yup.
acceptanceCriteria: Next.js 15 app with App Router initializes and runs with pnpm dev
All fonts (42 files), images, Lottie JSON files, media, and documents copied from source repo
SCSS variables, mixins, reset, fonts, custom, and global styles are adapted and compile without errors
tsconfig.json has baseUrl './src' with appropriate path aliases
pnpm run build succeeds with no errors

stories.title: Content Extraction & Static Data Files
stories.description: Extract all content from the source repo (/Users/gustavo/taiko/website-v3). Extract translation JSON from /public/locales/en/ into static TypeScript data files. Create content files for page data (home, alethia, gwyneth, gwyneth-apps, dao) in src/content/pages/. Create navigation data file (header + footer nav structure) in src/content/data/navigation.ts. Create static data files for events, ecosystem projects, project categories, blog categories in src/content/data/. Create blog posts as MDX files with frontmatter in src/content/blog/. Create career positions as MDX files with frontmatter in src/content/careers/. Create content utility functions (getBlogs, getBlog, getCareers, getCareer, getEvents, getProjects, etc.) that read from static files.
acceptanceCriteria: All page content from source repo translation files is extracted into TypeScript data files
Navigation structure (header mega-menu groups + footer columns + social links) is in src/content/data/navigation.ts
Blog posts exist as MDX files with proper frontmatter in src/content/blog/
Career positions exist as MDX files with proper frontmatter in src/content/careers/
Events, ecosystem projects, project categories, blog categories are in src/content/data/
Content utility functions exist and are importable
TypeScript compiles with no errors

stories.title: Shared UI Components
stories.description: Port all shared UI components from the source repo (/Users/gustavo/taiko/website-v3). Components to port: Button, ArrowButton, BackLink, Accordion, Input, Select, Select-2, MultiSelect, Modal (with provider), FileInput, DragFile, Image (Next.js Image wrapper), NextLink (Next Link wrapper), ShareButton, MediaQuery (responsive conditional rendering), MediaLoader (lazy loading), Sprite (SVG sprite renderer), SubscribeSection (newsletter form), Socials (social media icons), Label, TextSplitter, LinkDropdown, RangeCalendar, RichText (replaces Strapi Blocks renderer — render MDX/markdown content), AlertCard, Banner, Grants. Use SCSS Modules for styling. Adapt from Pages Router patterns to App Router (remove withTranslation HOCs, use 'use client' where needed).
acceptanceCriteria: All listed UI components are ported with SCSS Module styling
Components use 'use client' directive where needed for interactivity
No withTranslation or i18n HOC dependencies remain
RichText component can render markdown/MDX content
TypeScript compiles with no errors

stories.title: Utilities, Hooks & Animation Setup
stories.description: Port utility functions and custom hooks from the source repo (/Users/gustavo/taiko/website-v3). Utilities: animate helpers, AOS (Animate On Scroll — adds '--animate' class when elements enter viewport), formatters, file helpers, sticky scroll. Custom hooks: useMediaQuery, useSearchParams, useScrollProgress, useForm. Animation setup: Register GSAP ScrollTrigger globally (client-side only), port SequentialDots animation component, set up Lottie player integration. Port SVG sprite system. All GSAP-dependent code must use 'use client' directive and dynamic imports where needed.
acceptanceCriteria: All utility functions are ported and importable
Custom hooks (useMediaQuery, useSearchParams, useScrollProgress, useForm) work correctly
GSAP ScrollTrigger registers on client side without SSR errors
AOS utility adds animation classes when elements enter viewport
SequentialDots animation component renders correctly
SVG sprite system works
TypeScript compiles with no errors
